milestone: MIXTRAL-CERT-3 (C5 active-path -> L3; built on C1+C2+C4)
headline: MoE-certified L3 (whole model) = active-path-certified
headline_note: Per ADR-007 the bare word 'certified' is forbidden for MoE. This family is 'MoE-certified L3' = active-path-certified = L2 + C5. C5 (active-path): Atenia's REAL full forward of the REAL trained Mixtral-8x7B-v0.1 (GQA attention + top-2 MoE, all 32 layers, via the disk expert-tier) on a canonical input, compared end-to-end against an external float64 reference computed ONE DECODER LAYER AT A TIME (HF attention in f64 + manual MoE one active expert at a time in f64) -- the F64 form of C5 over the ACTIVE SUBGRAPH, never the whole model in F64. Result: worst end-to-end max_abs_diff 3.185e-4 < 0.5 (ADR-004 bar, unchanged; ~1570x inside), per-position argmax EXACT 4/4, deterministic (two forwards bit-identical). Underlying L2: C1 (per-expert) exhaustive over ALL 32 layers x 8 experts = 256 experts + C2 (router top-2 SET EQUALITY) on all 32 layers, both on the REAL weights vs f64; C3 (GQA attention) + C4 (assembly/topology) via the mixtral_scale mechanism cert (1.639e-7). This is L3 (active-path-certified), NOT the dense ADR-004 CERTIFIED, and NOT L4 (global F64). L3 = 'the real model, as actually run on the active path, matches an external f64 oracle'.

C1_per_expert.scope: ALL 32 layers x 8 experts = 256 experts (exhaustive; all layers are MoE, no dense-first, no shared expert)
C1_per_expert.weights: real trained Mixtral-8x7B-v0.1 weights (bf16, 19 safetensors shards)
C1_per_expert.oracle: NumPy/torch float64, one expert at a time, one layer at a time (no full-model F64)
C1_per_expert.metric: max_abs_diff(Atenia expert forward, F64 reference) per expert, single deterministic probe input
C1_per_expert.gate: max_abs_diff < 0.5 for EVERY expert (ADR-004 bar, unchanged)
C1_per_expert.result: all 256 experts < 0.5 (global worst ~1.9e-6, ~2.6e5x inside the gate)

C2_router.hard_gate: top-2 expert SET EQUALITY vs the F64 reference, every layer
C2_router.routing_margin_note: gap between the 2nd and 3rd router logit per layer; the tightest layer (13, margin 0.0114) still matched exactly. norm_topk_prob renorm does not affect the top-k SET.
C2_router.result: top-2 set identical on all 32 layers

C3_attention.evidence: Mixtral topology full-forward cert (mixtral_scale 1.639e-7 vs HF, tests/moe_scale_cert_test.rs::mixtral_8x7b_topology_certifies) -- a reduced-dim full transformer incl. GQA attention + RoPE + the MoE block
C3_attention.caveat: certifies the GQA attention MECHANISM (tiny topology fixture), NOT the real-weight per-layer attention re-certified. Same standard as Qwen-MoE / DeepSeek C3. Recorded, not over-claimed.

C4_assembly_topology.topology: a reduced-dim Mixtral-8x7B topology end-to-end with the EXACT conventions: 8 experts / top-2 / GQA 4:1 (8 heads, 2 KV heads) / classic block_sparse_moe experts (w1/w3/w2) / no shared expert / renorm top-k, at reduced hidden dim (64) and 2 layers
C4_assembly_topology.gate: end-to-end max_abs_diff < 0.5 (ADR-004 bar, unchanged); clears it (also clears the 1e-3 test bound by ~6100x)
C4_assembly_topology.result: PASS -- the routing + combine + assembly + GQA-attention mechanism is certified end-to-end vs HF f64 on the Mixtral-8x7B topology (1.639e-7)
C4_assembly_topology.caveat: C4 certifies the MECHANISM on a reduced-dim, RANDOM-weight topology fixture -- NOT the real 8-expert trained-weight assembly. Per ADR-007 it transfers to the real model only IN CONJUNCTION WITH the whole-model C1/C2 (real weights), which are met. Recorded, not over-claimed.

C5_active_path.scope: Atenia's real full forward of the real trained Mixtral-8x7B-v0.1 (embeddings + 32 decoder layers of GQA attention + RoPE + top-2 MoE + final norm + lm_head) on a canonical input, end-to-end vs an external f64 reference
C5_active_path.weights: real trained Mixtral-8x7B-v0.1 weights (bf16, 19 safetensors shards), experts streamed from the disk expert-tier (ATENIA_MOE_EXPERT_TIER=disk; persistent bf16 tier)
C5_active_path.oracle: float64 reference computed ONE DECODER LAYER AT A TIME: HF MixtralAttention in float64 (q/k/v/o only) + manual MoE computing ONE ACTIVE EXPERT AT A TIME in float64 (router softmax in f32 -> top-2 -> renorm -> f64; expert = w2(silu(w1.x)*(w3.x))). The F64 form of C5 over the ACTIVE SUBGRAPH -- never the whole model in F64 (that would be L4). Driver validated against the mixtral_scale tiny fixture (2.021e-8 vs HF f64) before use.
C5_active_path.metric: end-to-end max_abs_diff(Atenia logits, F64 reference logits), worst over all positions x vocab (32000)
C5_active_path.gate: end-to-end max_abs_diff < 0.5 (ADR-004 bar, unchanged); clears it by ~1570x
C5_active_path.deterministic: yes -- two consecutive Atenia forwards are bit-identical (assert_eq)
C5_active_path.timing: load (warm tier reconstruct) 4.5s; forward 402.7s (CPU, bounded expert cache, disk-tier re-reads); test wall 955.74s
C5_active_path.result: PASS -- the real model, exactly as Atenia runs it on the active path, matches the f64 oracle (worst 3.185e-4 < 0.5, argmax exact 4/4, deterministic) -> ADR-007 L3

pending_levels.blocker: global model.double() F64 (~374 GB) -- reserved / unreachable; never fabricated.

remaining_caveats: C3 attention reused at the mechanism level (tiny topology fixture), not real-weight per-layer (same standard as Qwen/DeepSeek).
C1/C2 use a single deterministic probe input applied to every layer's real weights (representative activation, not a real inter-layer trace) -- consistent with the Qwen / DeepSeek approach.
C4 (mixtral_scale topology) is certified at the MECHANISM level (reduced-dim, random-weight fixture); it transfers to the real model only in conjunction with the real-weight C1/C2 (met).
C5 is certified over the ACTIVE SUBGRAPH in f64 (the experts/positions actually exercised by the canonical input), not a global f64 forward; this is ADR-007's primary C5 method and is L3, not L4.
C5 uses one canonical input (input_ids [1,100,200,300], seq 4); it certifies the active path for that probe, the same single-probe standard as Qwen/DeepSeek C5.
L3 here still carries the C3+C4 mechanism caveat (attention/topology re-derived at the mechanism level, not real-weight per-layer); it is NOT the dense ADR-004 CERTIFIED, and NOT L4 (global F64).

verification.c1_c2_reference: fixtures/moe/mixtral_decomp_ref.safetensors (+ .json), float64 one expert at a time (~4.2 MB); generator fixtures/moe/generate_mixtral_decomposition_reference.py (resumable)
verification.c1_c2_command: python fixtures/moe/generate_mixtral_decomposition_reference.py models/Mixtral-8x7B-v0.1 fixtures/moe ; then MIXTRAL_DIR=models/Mixtral-8x7B-v0.1 cargo test --release --test moe_mixtral_decomposition_test -- --ignored mixtral_real_c1_c2 --nocapture
verification.c1_c2_expected: C1 global worst max_abs_diff ~1.9e-6 (< 0.5, all 256 experts, layer 8 / expert 1); C2 top-2 set match on all 32 layers; min routing margin 0.011413 (layer 13); 0 failures.
verification.c4_command: cargo test --release --test moe_scale_cert_test mixtral_8x7b_topology_certifies -- --nocapture  (expected end-to-end max_abs_diff 1.639e-7, argmax exact, greedy->EOS, deterministic)
verification.c5_harness: tests/moe_mixtral_c5_active_path_test.rs (mixtral_real_c5_active_path, #[ignore] + env MIXTRAL_DIR); gates worst max_abs_diff < 0.5 + per-position argmax exact + determinism, else does NOT certify L3 (asserts fail loudly).
verification.c5_reference: fixtures/moe/mixtral_c5_ref.safetensors (+ .json); generator fixtures/moe/generate_mixtral_c5_reference.py (modes: 'tiny' validates the driver vs mixtral_scale HF f64 = 2.021e-8; 'real <dir> <out>' emits the reference, resumable per-layer atomic hidden-state checkpoints, one decoder layer at a time in f64, active expert at a time).
verification.c5_command: python fixtures/moe/generate_mixtral_c5_reference.py real models/Mixtral-8x7B-v0.1 fixtures/moe ; then $env:MIXTRAL_DIR='models/Mixtral-8x7B-v0.1'; $env:ATENIA_MOE_EXPERT_TIER='disk'; $env:ATENIA_MOE_TIER_PERSIST='1'; cargo test --release --test moe_mixtral_c5_active_path_test -- --ignored mixtral_real_c5_active_path --nocapture
verification.c5_expected: MIXTRAL-C5 RESULT ... C5 PASS, worst max_abs_diff 3.185e-4 (pos 0) < 0.5, argmax exact 4/4 [422,327,160,327], deterministic.
verification.c5_operational_note: On a 32 GB host the forward MUST run with a BOUNDED expert cache (ATENIA_MOE_EXPERT_CACHE=1) -- numerically identical (caching is a perf optimisation only), but the default per-layer cache of (2*top_k)=4 reconstructed-F32 experts x 32 layers commits ~90 GB and exceeds the system commit limit -> OOM. With cache=1 the peak is ~29 GB (22 GB experts + ~6.5 GB backend) and fits. A persistent disk tier (ATENIA_MOE_TIER_PERSIST=1, ATENIA_DISK_TIER_DIR=<nvme>) makes the load a ~4.5s warm reconstruct (no shard re-read).

generated_on: F64 reference: torch (bf16->float64) + NumPy float64, Windows 11 (per-layer-resumable generator). Atenia C5 forward: CPU, 32 GB RAM host, model + persistent bf16 disk expert-tier on NVMe (SSD), bounded expert cache (ATENIA_MOE_EXPERT_CACHE=1); warm tier reconstruct 4.5s, forward 402.7s.
